真正引爆危机的是亚马逊。亚马逊作为Anthropic的主要投资方和合作伙伴，其研究人员对Fable 5进行了红队测试，发现了一个潜在的越狱（jailbreak）漏洞。亚马逊CEO安迪·贾西对这一发现感到担忧，直接致电了美国财政部长斯科特·贝森特。特朗普政府随即迅速反应，向Anthropic发出90分钟的最后通牒，要求立即关闭这两款模型，并规定“任何外国国民”都不得访问——无论该人员是否在Anthropic工作、是否在美国境内。Anthropic无法在极短时间内建立符合要求的访问控制机制，只得全面下线两款模型，公司CEO达里奥·阿莫代伊甚至因此未能及时回电。

核心技术创新点：Anthropic的Mythos系列代表了当前AI能力的顶尖水平。Fable 5本质上是Mythos 5的“安全护栏版”，公司宣称它具备前所未有的漏洞利用能力，但公众版本通过多层护栏设计限制了其潜在危害。然而，这种“双向护城河”策略引发了矛盾：安全研究者无法全面评估模型，而一旦发现潜在问题（即使其他模型如GPT-5.5也能实现类似功能），政府就会过度反应。

性能与对比数据：据独立红队测试人员反馈，Fable 5的护栏比预期更有效，他们在多次尝试后仍未成功突破。这与通常能快速找到漏洞的情况形成鲜明对比。但亚马逊研究团队还是发现了潜在漏洞，尽管双方研究人员已经就此事进行了数天的技术讨论，并未认为这是世界末日。更重要的是，行业内部人士指出，Fable 5被发现的潜在能力并非其独有，OpenAI的GPT-5.5等模型也能实现类似功能。因此，业界普遍质疑：“为什么偏偏是Anthropic？”

技术影响与应用场景：Fable和Mythos的核心应用场景包括：企业网络安全漏洞自动修补、政府关键基础设施保护、以及高级网络防御。Anthropic声称，这些模型可以“在恶意行为者利用漏洞之前，帮助企业修补所有框架”。然而，这种强大的能力也使其成为双刃剑。

这起事件的讽刺之处在于：Anthropic多年来一直积极呼吁政府加强对AI的监管，警告AI可能很快强大到危险的地步。但当监管真正到来时，公司却发现自己成了受害者。更重要的是，特朗普政府内部对AI的态度反复无常——一方面签署行政令推动美国AI出口、对抗中国，另一方面又突然以最严厉的方式限制自家的旗舰AI公司。一位知情人士评论说：“北京正在嘲笑我们。”有行业消息称，其他国家的企业已经开始签署与美国非捆绑的备用合同，部署开源权重模型和替代硬件方案，将“政治风险”纳入商业计划。

对于美国AI监管的未来，目前存在巨大不确定性。特朗普政府推翻了拜登时期的科技创新AI行政令，但似乎又通过各种非正式渠道重新走向某种形式的控制。Anthropic事件表明，缺乏制度建设、依赖个人关系的监管方式可能导致行业混乱。有迹象显示，政府可能正在考虑收购Anthropic和OpenAI的部分所有权，这使得局势更加复杂。业界普遍认为，针对“外国国民”的极端条款很可能在未来几天内被收回，但这次事件已经对美国的AI国际信任造成了损害。正如The Verge资深AI记者海登·菲尔德所说：“现在是时候给行业一个可预测的、严密的监管框架了，而不是让每个新模型都面临周末恐慌电话的考验。”

history.title: Ditching the cloud for local AI — how I use two mini PCs to process millions of tokens a day and save money on costly API fees
history.description: As new data center buildouts hit planning walls and AI inference providers hike costs, is the future of AI to roll your own models?
history.content: Share Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter For heavy AI users, the economics of the current boom are starting to bite. Over the past year, major labs have nudged prices upward while tightening the screws on usage — whether through stricter rate limits, smaller context windows on lower tiers, or the gradual reshuffling of features behind more expensive plans. Even where per-token costs have fallen in headline terms, the reality for users is more complicated: higher volumes, more complex workflows, and new tooling expectations mean monthly bills are creeping up, not down.At the same time, open-weight models have improved rapidly, consumer hardware has become more capable, and tools like LM Studio, Ollama, and llama.cpp have made local deployment far more accessible than it was even a year ago. The result is a renaissance in running models on your own machines.I’m one of the people who has taken the leap myself. In mid-March, I bought a GMKtech mini PC with an AMD Ryzen AI Max+ 395 chip and 96GB of RAM. The purchase — at the time something like £1,500 ($2,000) — was a calculated decision. The kinds of volume I wanted AI models to run through would have blown through my current subscriptions to AI models (I have a ChatGPT Plus and GLM Coding Lite plan, which combined cost me around $23 a month), and forced me onto the higher-cost monthly plans, or API-based inference.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Going localThe decision I had to make was a simple one: did I want to spend that money on a subscription that would cost me several thousand dollars over the course of a year, and end up having to pay a recurring cost for years to come to an AI lab that would likely hike prices? Or did I want to pay a one-off charge for my own hardware and a smaller ongoing cost for electricity?I chose the latter. You may like AI costs spike as subscriptions hit pricing wall Talent over tokens: AI models are becoming more expensive to run, and productivity gains are limited AI cost crisis hits tech giants as employee 'tokenmaxxing' backfires When the mini PC arrived, setting it up was relatively easy — though, fully disclosure, only possible with the help of the full-fat AI models I pay for from the big labs.The system I set up on my hardware was designed to try and help me keep track of the constantly changing news in the areas I cover for sites like Tom’s Hardware Premium and others. It takes RSS feeds and ingests the contents of stories in key beats that I cover, then grades them against a digital ‘brain’ made of how I think about the world and what I report on, generated by analyzing nearly 2,000 of my past stories over the previous four years.When it finds candidates that are potentially interesting, those stories are ‘assigned’ to AI beat reporters, who then read around the subject on the web and produce pitches, similar to those that I send to my editors here and elsewhere. Those AI reporters then send their pitches to AI editors, who engage in a conversation with the reporters to fine-tune the idea’s framing, before presenting me with a couple of paragraphs of a broad idea that is meant to be tailored to my tastes via Telegram. The outputs are far from perfect — I’d equate them to a newly-graduated student that I teach in terms of their taste and depth — but they’re a good starting point for me to learn about what’s important on a given day, and a provocation for how I might think about framing those events. For the kind of things I’m using AI for, even the bleeding-edge frontier models aren’t much better than the local LLM options, though I appreciate that there’s a bigger gap when thinking about coding.SetupThe whole process uses LM Studio and runs on a mix of quantized models, generally of Qwen3.5 and 3.6. Because I’m running multiple editor and reporter processes in parallel, the parameter count on each model may seem undersized for the 96GB of RAM that my AMD GPU can access (after some BIOS tweaks): I’m using a mix of Qwen’s straightforward 3.5-9B model, as well as Jackrong’s Qwen-3.5-9B-GLM-5.1-Distilled and Qwopus-3.5-9B models. In part, that’s because thousands of calls on the models take place every day, and in order to keep on top of the backlog of stories to look through and ‘discuss,’ throughput needs to be high.Since starting the locally-hosted project in mid-March, my local LLMs have burned through anywhere between 20 million and 50 million tokens a day alone. (Alongside troubleshooting with paid-for and hosted models, as well as parallel projects I run on my GLM Coding plan subscription, I’m using between 50-100 million tokens on an average day.) What to read next AI costs begin to bite as agents may increase token demand by 24 times, says Goldman Sachs report Demand for data center CPUs has surged, and AI agents are responsible OpenAI CEO Sam Altman admits AI token costs are becoming 'a huge issue.' For this kind of reading, thinking, analyzing, and re-presenting, local models work brilliantly. They have high throughput but are working in the background, meaning that the slower time to first token that many local LLM users complain about in comparison to big lab-hosted alternatives isn’t an issue for me. The model runs 24 hours a day, and if it takes two seconds or two minutes to process the prompts (between 7,000 and 18,000 tokens, depending on whether it’s a reporter or editor and how far through the discussion process it is), it doesn’t bother me. Tokens per second won’t impress those talking a big game about local LLMs on social media: the models handle the prompts at around 300 tok/s, while the output is a much slower 5-10 tok/s. Yet it works for me.Split throughput But for now, I’m still keeping my big lab subscriptions — though I’m using them differently. My GLM Coding plan, bought around Christmastime and which lasts for a year, is used alongside Codex through my OpenAI subscription to troubleshoot and tinker with the projects when issues arise. My coding knowledge stopped at some QuickBASIC and Delphi in my teenage years, so having the ability to call on them (and an OpenCode Go subscription I occasionally dip into) to fix problems is invaluable.However, the proportion of my AI use has shifted significantly. Two-thirds or more of my total token use is now locally-hosted LLMs I run myself. And as local models continue to develop their abilities and the gap between them and the state of the art from big labs closes, I can envisage that it will increase. For instance, I recently vibe-coded a web interface for LM Studio that allows me to use it as a regular chatbot just this last week. And in just two months, the amount I’ve saved if I had run that project every day through API calls on GPT-5.4-mini, arguably a comparable model, is three-quarters of the cost of that first mini PC — around $1,500.In hindsight, I wish I’d bought the 128GB version of my mini PC, which is why I decided around two weeks ago, before another memory-based price hike, to buy the bigger version. The reason was a simple one: the volume of queries I was putting through my 96GB box was starting to hit the limits, and I wanted to expand the project. I also wanted to test out locally hosted coding harnesses like Claude Code or Hermes using a local model.The experience, trials, and tribulations from my first mini PC setup helped enormously with setting up the second PC. Token count has increased from 20-50 million tokens a day to more like 50-80 million tokens a day. I offloaded part of that massive ingest and analysis project onto the new hardware and put it onto more powerful 27B and 36B parameter models (through the Final-Bench-Darwin-36B-Opus model), freeing up space on my first mini PC and allowing me to test the idea of a locally-hosted Claude Code-style project with the spare space on my second mini PC.That has been less successful — at least so far. Underpinning the coding harness with GLM-4.7-Flash works, but feels like too big a step back in model generations to be a useful tradeoff. Larger Qwen models have so far got stuck in their own thinking (or burned through a lot of the context window they’re assigned), but I’m considering swapping Claude Code out for a lighter-weight, less context-heavy harness and giving it a proper run.Frontier models are getting more expensiveThe bet I’ve made is a simple one: subscription and API prices from frontier labs — with the odd outlier like DeepSeek excepted — are only going to go in one direction as the companies behind them realize they need to make a financial return for investors. Even if prices don’t go into the stratosphere, labs might make tradeoffs to cut down on usage — as we’ve already seen GitHub doing. And while the race to build capacity to meet demand for those major AI labs will continue to push up prices for hardware in the short term, I still think it’s a better bet to have control over your own models and how much you pay for them than to leave it in the hands of big companies.So I’ll keep tinkering with my local stack, which has already gone from one mini PC to two interlinked ones — and already have my eyes on a PC with an Nvidia GPU to give me the token speed that’s currently missing. But for now, I think it’s worth keeping what I have for a while and seeing how I can eke out additional benefits before making the leap financially in expanding my whole system.
history.author: Chris Stokel-Walker
history.titleZh: 放弃云端转向本地AI——我如何用两台迷你PC每天处理数百万tokens并节省昂贵的API费用
history.descriptionZh:
对于重度AI用户来说，当前AI热潮的经济压力正日益显现。过去一年里，各大AI实验室在抬高价格的同时，还通过更严格的速率限制、低层级服务更小的上下文窗口，或将功能逐步转移到更昂贵的套餐中，悄然收紧使用条件。即便每token的标称成本有所下降，但实际情况对用户而言更为复杂：更高的处理量、更复杂的工作流以及对新工具能力的期待，使得月账单不降反升。

与此同时，开源权重模型快速进步，消费级硬件性能增强，LM Studio、Ollama、llama.cpp等工具让本地部署变得比一年前容易得多。结果就是，在自己的机器上运行模型迎来复兴。

我本人就是这一趋势的践行者。三月中旬，我购买了一台搭载AMD Ryzen AI Max+ 395芯片和96GB内存的GMKtech迷你电脑，花费约1500英镑（2000美元）。这是一次经过计算的决策：我想要的AI模型运行量，会轻易突破我现有的订阅计划（我订阅了ChatGPT Plus和GLM Coding Lite，月费共约23美元），迫使我转向更高成本的月付计划或基于API的推理。我的选择很简单：是每年花数千美元订阅，且未来数年都要向可能涨价的AI实验室支付经常性费用？还是一次性支付硬件费用，加上较小的持续电费？我选择了后者。

当迷你电脑到货后，设置过程相对简单——但坦白说，只有借助我从大实验室付费使用的完整AI模型才能完成。我在硬件上搭建的系统，旨在帮助我跟踪我所报道领域（如Tom's Hardware Premium等）不断变化的新闻。它接收RSS源，摄取我主要关注板块的报道内容，然后根据一个由我过去四年近2000篇报道生成的、代表我世界观和报道方式的数字“大脑”对它们进行评分。当发现潜在有趣的候选文章时，这些故事会被“分配”给AI记者，它们会在网络上阅读相关主题并撰写简报，类似我发送给编辑的提案。这些AI记者随后将简报发送给AI编辑，编辑与记者进行对话以微调报道框架，最后通过Telegram将涵盖核心想法的几段文字呈现给我。输出远非完美——我将其比作刚毕业、在我指导下学习的学生——但它们是了解每日重要事件的良好起点，也是思考如何报道这些事件的启发。对于我使用AI的这类任务，即使是前沿模型也不比本地LLM好太多，尽管在编程方面差距更大。

整个系统使用LM Studio，运行在混合的量化模型上，主要是Qwen3.5和3.6。由于我并行运行多个编辑和记者进程，每个模型的参数量看似对我的96GB内存（经过BIOS调整后，AMD GPU可访问）来说偏小：我同时使用了Qwen的3.5-9B标准模型，以及Jackrong的Qwen-3.5-9B-GLM-5.1-Distilled和Qwopus-3.5-9B模型。部分原因是每天有数千次模型调用，为了处理积压的待审故事并保持讨论流程，吞吐量必须很高。

自三月中旬启动本地项目以来，我的本地LLM每天单独消耗2000万到5000万token。（加上使用付费托管模型进行故障排查，以及并行运行的GLM Coding计划项目，我平均每天使用5000万到1亿token。）对于这种阅读、思考、分析和重新呈现的任务，本地模型表现出色。它们拥有高吞吐量且后台运行，因此许多本地LLM用户抱怨的相比大实验室托管模型的首次token延迟较长，对我来说不是问题。模型24小时运行，如果处理提示（根据角色和讨论阶段，每次7000到18000 token）需要两秒或两分钟，我都不介意。token每秒速度不会让那些在社交媒体上吹嘘本地LLM的人印象深刻：模型处理提示约300 tok/s，而输出则慢得多，只有5-10 tok/s。但这对我有效。

目前我仍保留着大实验室的订阅，但使用方式不同。我在圣诞节前后购买的GLM Coding计划（有效期一年）与OpenAI订阅中的Codex配合使用，在项目出现问题时进行调试和修整。我的编程知识停留在青少年时期的QuickBASIC和Delphi，因此能够调用这些工具（以及偶尔使用的OpenCode Go订阅）来解决问题非常宝贵。然而，我的AI使用比例发生了显著变化：总token使用量的三分之二或更多现在由本地LLM承担。随着本地模型能力的持续发展，它们与大实验室前沿模型之间的差距不断缩小，我预计这一比例还会增加。例如，我最近为一个让LM Studio可以像常规聊天机器人一样使用的网页界面进行了“vibe coding”。仅仅两个月，如果我通过GPT-5.4-mini（可与之比较的模型）的API调用来每天运行该项目，节省的费用就已达到第一台迷你电脑成本的四分之三——约1500美元。

事后看来，我希望当初购买的是128GB版本的迷你电脑，这也是我在大约两周前、另一次内存涨价之前决定购买更大版本的原因。原因很简单：我对96GB设备发起的查询量开始触及极限，而且我想扩展项目。我还想用本地模型测试Claude Code或Hermes等本地编程工具。第一台迷你电脑的经验和教训极大地帮助了第二台的设置。token数量从每天2000万-5000万增加到每天5000万-8000万。我将部分庞大的摄入和分析项目转移到新硬件上，并换用更强大的27B和36B参数模型（通过Final-Bench-Darwin-36B-Opus模型），释放了第一台迷你电脑的空间，同时允许我在第二台迷你电脑上测试本地托管的Claude Code风格项目。这一尝试至少到目前为止不太成功——用GLM-4.7-Flash支撑编程工具虽可行，但感觉模型代际差距太大，不值得交换。更大的Qwen模型目前陷入自身思考（或消耗了过多分配的上下文窗口），但我正在考虑将Claude Code换成一个更轻量、上下文开销更小的工具，并正式运行。

我的赌注很简单：前沿实验室的订阅和API价格（除DeepSeek等少数例外）只会朝一个方向走——因为这些公司意识到需要对投资者实现财务回报。即使价格不暴涨，实验室也可能通过限制使用量来做出权衡——正如我们已经看到GitHub的做法。虽然为满足那些主要AI实验室需求而进行的容量竞赛短期内会推高硬件价格，但我仍然认为，控制自己的模型及其成本，比将主动权交给大公司是更好的赌注。因此，我将继续调整我的本地设施，它已从一台迷你电脑发展成两台互联设备——并且我已经将目光投向一台搭载Nvidia GPU的PC，以获得目前缺失的token速度。但现在，我认为值得继续使用现有设备一段时间，看看在财务上迈出扩张整体系统的步伐之前，还能榨出哪些额外收益。

history.title: Noctua NL-LC1-36 Review: Compromise paves the way
history.description: We test Noctua’s first attempt at an all-in-one liquid cooler, focusing specifically on pump performance to determine whether Noctua’s AIO is truly a viable alternative to swapping the fans on another unit and the brand’s own legendary air cooler.
history.content: For a very long time, many of us have been begging Noctua to build a liquid cooler. In a time when CPUs are becoming ever more powerful, their power consumption is also rising, and air-coolers are often no longer up to the task. However, this was a problem for Noctua’s customers, as the brand has stubbornly not built a liquid cooler – at least until now. Meet the NL-LC1-36, Noctua’s first liquid cooler.Today we’re reviewing the 360-mm variant, but it also comes in 240mm and 420mm flavors. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Let’s start with the elephant in the room: At its core, this is not a liquid cooler designed 100% in-house by Noctua – it’s an Asetek cooler with some Noctua touches. This may be a bit of a let-down, especially for Noctua purists – up until now, every product that had a name in Noctua’s nomenclature, such as NL-LC1-36, or NH-D15 G2, or NF-A12x25 G2, was 100% designed and built in-house by Rascom and Kolink, the two owners of the Noctua brand.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Noctua NL-LC1-36 at Amazon for $249.95Keen readers will note that there is a Noctua case, Noctua PSU, and Noctua GPUs, but that’s not entirely accurate. The Antec Flux Pro Noctua Edition, Seasonic Prime TX-1600 Noctua Edition, and Asus Noctua Edition GPUs are all, as their name implies, Noctua-Edition products, which are not strictly speaking a core part of Noctua’s own lineup, and they do not carry Noctua’s typical nomenclature.What’s really going on here is something we’ll get into later, but for now, let’s dive into the box and see what we’ve got on the test bench.SpecificationsSwipe to scroll horizontallyCoolerNoctua NL-LC1-36ColorsBlack & BrownCompatibilityAMD AM5, AM4Intel 1700, 1851Radiator Dimensions400 (L) x 120mm (W) x 30mm (H)Fans3x NF-A12x25 G2Fan Speed300 – 1800 RPMPump Speed750 – 3400 RPMMSRP$249.99Warranty6 yearsRow 9 - Cell 0 Row 9 - Cell 1 Today's best Noctua NL-LC1-36 deals $249.95View No price informationCheck Best BuyWe check over 250 million products every day for the best pricesProduct Walkthrough (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The cooler comes packaged in a typical Noctua box with a matte finish and product details written on it. Noctua also sells an auxiliary fan, the NL-ACF1, which can clip onto the pump block to cool the area surrounding the CPU. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Open the box, and we’re presented with a nice appetizer – the large Noctua plaque that can later be installed onto the pump block if you’re not using the auxiliary fan.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)This is a truly beautiful plaque, and quite large too – a bit unusually large for Noctua. Is Noctua trying to emphasize its own brand to distract us from Asetek?Image 1 of 2(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)An included accessory box contains the mounting brackets, cleaning wipe, instructions, TIM guard, and the classic Noctua Torx screwdriver. Both the AMD and intel mounting brackets come with -7mm offset holes so that the cooler is better placed over the CPU’s hotspots. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The three fans come in their own box too. Here we have three NF-A12x25 G2s, two of which are the PPA variant and one PPB, which have 50 RPM offsets to prevent harmonics from forming between them. These fans spin at such consistent speeds, that if you mount two next to each other on the same fan splitter, they can sing in harmony. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The fans all come equipped with the full rear gasket for a perfect seal on the radiator. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Moving on to the pump assembly, here we find the pièce de résistance, the NL-PNA1. PNA stands for Pump Noise Absorber, and this is a custom pump cover designed by Noctua that features multiple layers of foam, sound barriers, and silicone mounts, all to quiet down the pump.At its heart, the NL-CL1 is based on Asetek’s Emma Gen8 V2 pump platform that, although very powerful, isn’t the quietest pump on the market. However, the combination of its power and the PNA1 cap means that Noctua is happy to stand behind the product, and we’ll see in testing whether this stance is justified.This pump cap isn’t only engineered to absorb and deflect noise, however – it’s also a tuned mass damper. Sitting on silicone mounts, its own mass and positioning is engineered such that it dampens mechanical noise. This damping essentially shifts the higher, more annoying frequencies pumps typically produce toward deeper, more pleasant frequencies that typically bother human hearing less. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The cold plate is beautifully milled, and the exposed copper should do a great job at transferring heat. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Hidden between the two tubes is a dip-switch with three positions. They’re not marked, but it’s easy to tell whether you’re in the quiet, balanced, or unrestricted mode. These limit the pump’s RPMs to 2100 or 2600 for the quiet and balanced profiles. Within these profiles, you can use the motherboard’s PWM control to guide the pump. However, the pump’s internal coolant temperature sensor will override the PWM signal at certain duty cycles or beyond certain coolant temperatures and push to 100% duty anyway, which may lead to the pump running louder than you want. In practice, however, we haven’t found this to be a problem.For full control, you’ll want to use the unrestricted mode, where you have the pump’s full 3700 RPM at your disposal. (Image credit: Noctua)What I find a bit of a let-down here, is that there is no two-pin thermal probe output. I have a lot of experience with custom water cooling, and my preferred method of managing pump and fan speeds is by using the coolant temperature as the source value. It’s great that this AIO’s pump will manage itself and I’m stoked to see this feature finally coming to numerous AIOs, but I’d like access to the coolant temperature so that the fans can be controlled by this too. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The AIO’s radiator is a relatively standard aluminum type, but it’s 30mm thick which is an improvement over thinner 25mm radiators on more budget-friendly coolers.Lastly, the loop is filled with Glycol, and is not user-serviceable. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Optionally, for $20, you can add an auxiliary fan to cool the VRM and memory surrounding the CPU, but this honestly feels like an afterthought and doesn’t look very charming compared to the rest of the cooler. I don’t doubt that it works, but it’s not nearly as neatly integrated into the pump block as we’ve seen with much of Noctua’s premium competition.Noctua NL-LC1-36: Price Comparison $249.95View No price informationCheck Best BuyWe check over 250 million products every day for the best pricespowered by

history.titleZh: 猫头鹰NL-LC1-36评测：妥协铺平道路
history.descriptionZh:
长期以来，CPU性能飙升的同时功耗也水涨船高，传统风冷逐渐力不从心。面对用户的反复呼吁，Noctua终于打破“不做水冷”的坚持，推出了首款一体式水冷散热器——NL-LC1-36，提供240mm、360mm和420mm三种规格。不过，这款产品并非Noctua完全自主研发：其核心液冷平台源自Asetek的Emma Gen8 V2泵方案，Noctua在此基础上进行了深度定制和调校。对品牌“原教旨主义者”而言，这或许有些遗憾，但Noctua的独特设计赋予了它鲜明的个性。

技术层面，NL-LC1-36的亮点在于一系列消音与振动优化。泵盖（NL-PNA1，Pump Noise Absorber）是“点睛之笔”：它集成了多层泡沫、隔音屏障和硅胶支架，既是物理隔音屏障，又是一个调谐质量阻尼器——利用自身质量和弹性安装位置，将泵体机械振动的高频噪声向人耳更不敏感的较低频率迁移。泵体本身采用Asetek Gen8 V2平台，虽非市面上最安静的方案，但配合这个泵盖后，Noctua对其实际表现充满自信。泵体还设有三档物理DIP开关（静音/平衡/无限制），分别将泵转速限制在2100 RPM、2600 RPM或放开至3700 RPM；同时内置冷却液温度传感器，可在特定占空比或水温下强制覆盖PWM信号至全速，不过实际使用中很少造成困扰。

风扇配置同样考究：三把NF-A12x25 G2风扇，其中两把为PPA变体、一把为PPB变体，二者有50 RPM的转速偏移差，防止相邻风扇因速度完全一致而产生谐振。所有风扇均配备完整后密封垫圈，与30mm厚的铝制散热器形成完美气密贴合。冷头铜底经精细铣削，裸露铜表面确保高效导热。安装扣具同时支持AMD AM5/AM4和Intel LGA1700/1851，并设有-7mm偏移孔位，使冷头更精准地覆盖CPU热点。

性能参数方面：360mm版本官方建议零售价249.99美元，提供6年质保。风扇转速范围300–1800 RPM，泵转速范围750–3400 RPM（受DIP档位限制）。附件包含标志性的Torx螺丝刀、清洁布、导热膏和冷头装饰铭牌（可替换辅助风扇安装位）。值得一提的是，Noctua还提供了售价20美元的辅助风扇NL-ACF1，可夹在泵块上额外为CPU周边VRM和内存区域散热，但集成度一般，外观不如核心组件优雅。

一个显著的遗憾是：冷头没有提供两针热敏探头输出接口。对于熟悉定制水冷的用户而言，最理想的控制逻辑是使用冷却液温度作为风扇转速的参考源——虽然泵体已内置传感器来自动管理自身转速，但无法将这个温度信号传递给主板用于控制散热器风扇，意味着用户仍需依赖CPU核心温度或自定义风扇曲线。

这款液冷的技术影响在于：Noctua首次将自身的声学优化哲学带入一体式水冷领域，通过物理手段而非单纯依赖低转速或被动降噪，展示了一条异于竞品的“安静高性能”路径。应用场景明确面向高端风冷已显吃力的高功耗CPU（如顶级Intel/AMD处理器），尤其适合对噪声敏感、又追求散热余量的用户。它证明了一个品牌即使采用第三方泵方案，也能通过精密附件和系统调校实现差异化竞争。未来如果Noctua能在完全自研的液冷方案上更进一步，或者将冷却液温度传感器接口开放给用户，这套产品线将拥有更完整的生态控制能力。

history.title: At Cannes Lions, NVIDIA Partners Reshape Advertising and Marketing With AI
history.description: The digital era gave the advertising and marketing industry speed; the AI era is giving it autonomous operations.  For companies building next-generation technologies for advertising and marketing, the question is no longer whether to adopt AI but whether their infrastructure can support it at the speed and scale the industry demands.  At Cannes Lions, running […]
history.content: The digital era gave the advertising and marketing industry speed; the AI era is giving it autonomous operations. For companies building next-generation technologies for advertising and marketing, the question is no longer whether to adopt AI but whether their infrastructure can support it at the speed and scale the industry demands. At Cannes Lions, running June 22-26 in France, industry leaders including Alembic, Amazon Web Services (AWS), Criteo, Higgsfield, KERV.ai and Taboola are showcasing how NVIDIA technologies help unlock greater creativity and enable faster, autonomous operations at enterprise scale. Decision Intelligence at Enterprise Scale Causal AI platform Alembic helps solve one of enterprises’ biggest challenges: proving what marketing initiatives actually drive growth, not just reporting on what happened. Modeling true causation simultaneously across every channel, market and audience requires AI infrastructure that can process enormous, fast-changing datasets without reducing them to correlation-based assumptions. NVIDIA DGX Vera Rubin NVL72 systems enable Alembic to scale its Causal AI models to analyze more variables, run larger simulations and quantify the true drivers of growth across marketing investments. Alembic will be the first Causal AI company to use NVIDIA DGX Vera Rubin SuperPODs for enterprise-scale causal modeling, giving executives a single source of unbiased truth on what drove business outcomes and where capital is being wasted, so they can act with confidence on future decisions. Alembic’s inference runs on private supercomputing infrastructure inside Equinix data centers where the enterprise data already lives, keeping AI workloads local. World Wide Technology extends this to secure and regulated environments. Together, the companies offer a complete enterprise AI stack purpose-built for executives and data leaders accountable for capital decisions. Smarter Bidding at Auction Speed For advertisers, serving ads and relevant recommendations across billions of daily transactions requires AI that’s accurate, fast and affordable enough to run at scale. Amazon Web Services (AWS) is bringing cloud infrastructure, foundation models and NVIDIA GPU-accelerated computing together into a cohesive stack for the adtech industry that can scale for the era of AI agents. AWS is giving advertisers and demand-side platforms, supply-side platforms and independent software vendors a production-ready reference implementation to run AI-powered bidding directly inside auctions — powered by NVIDIA Triton Inference Server, which delivers deep learning inference fast enough to fit within real-time auction windows. That means adtech companies can move from rules-based decisioning to AI-powered models for bid price optimization, audience activation and deal scoring directly within the live auction pipeline. Advertising company Criteo helps retailers show the right product to the right shopper at the right moment, across one of the largest recommendation networks in digital advertising. Keeping those recommendations relevant means continuously retraining its AI on billions of shopper timelines, a process where speed directly translates to quality. Collaborating with NVIDIA, Criteo achieved a roughly 2x speedup in model training on NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs, driven by the NVIDIA cuEmbed open library. That efficiency already frees roughly 17,000 GPU hours a year, and the companies are now scaling the work further. Taboola is applying the same infrastructure logic to conversational AI, using NVIDIA GPUs to power DeeperDive, its AI answer engine, and extending that infrastructure to AI platforms and chatbots so they can generate revenue from advertising. Agentic AI Across the Marketing Workflow In marketing and other industries, AI agents are increasingly acting as digital coworkers, taking on long-running tasks across planning, execution and optimization. But these agents are only deployable for enterprises when they come with proper controls, including safety guardrails, auditability and role-based permissioning. The NVIDIA Agent Toolkit, which includes NVIDIA NemoClaw blueprints and the NVIDIA OpenShell secure runtime, provides these controls. For example, Higgsfield AI, an AI video and image generator production platform, offers Higgsfield Supercomputer agents that manage the full marketing automation lifecycle: from campaign ideation, planning, creative production to posting and autonomous campaign optimization — in a single interface. It orchestrates leading large language models alongside 35+ image, audio and video models, including Higgsfield’s proprietary Soul and Soul 2.0 models built on NVIDIA Blackwell architecture. As part of the collaboration, NVIDIA Agent Toolkit software, including NVIDIA Nemotron open models, powers specialized subagents within the Higgsfield Supercomputer, running continuously inside every campaign. NemoClaw and OpenShell are being integrated to provide the enterprise trust layer. The result: the full marketing lifecycle, from ideation and creative production through posting, performance analysis and optimization, is available in a single interface. Marketing campaigns for nearly 400 of the Fortune 500 companies are created on the platform. https://blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/06/higgsfield-agent-workflow.mp4 Video courtesy of Higgsfield. Contextual and Content Intelligence at Scale AI understanding content at the level of meaning requires advanced infrastructure. NVIDIA’s multimodal stack provides the vector search, data processing and video understanding capabilities that make this kind of intelligence viable at production scale. AI-powered media leader KERV’s Moment Match Engine evaluates a multitude of signals across every video frame and media asset to understand individual scenes, objects and products, providing content recommendations based on ad creative — the visual and textual elements of an advertisement — to drive improved engagement. https://blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/06/KERV-metadata-motiongraphic-fix.mp4 Video courtesy of KERV.ai. KERV.ai recently optimized its processing pipeline, achieving over 10x improvements in speed and efficiency when using the NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Nano Omni open model in the platform. KERV’s solution analyzes what each ad or media brief contains, who it resonates with and which exact moment within content environments to target. On MediaPerf, an open benchmark for AI video understanding, Nemotron 3 Nano Omni — adopted by ecosystem partners including PYLER, which uses NVIDIA DGX B200 systems — delivered the highest throughput and lowest inference cost of any model evaluated, open or closed source. Learn more about how NVIDIA powers advertising and marketing technologies. Featured video courtesy of Higgsfield.
history.author: Jamie Allan
history.titleZh: 在戛纳国际创意节上，英伟达合作伙伴借助AI重塑广告与营销
history.descriptionZh:
数字时代为广告营销行业带来了速度，而AI时代正在赋予其自主运营能力。对于构建新一代广告营销技术的公司而言，问题不再是是否采用AI，而是其基础设施能否支持行业所需的速度和规模。在6月22日至26日于法国举办的戛纳国际创意节上，包括Alembic、亚马逊云科技、Criteo、Higgsfield、KERV.ai和Taboola在内的行业领导者，正在展示NVIDIA技术如何解锁更大的创造力，并实现企业级更快速、更自主的运营。

**企业级决策智能**

因果AI平台Alembic致力于解决企业面临的最大挑战之一：证明哪些营销举措真正推动了增长，而不仅仅是报告发生了什么。要同时在每个渠道、市场和受众中建模真正的因果关系，需要能够处理庞大且快速变化的数据集，同时避免降级为基于相关性的假设的AI基础设施。NVIDIA DGX Vera Rubin NVL72系统使Alembic能够扩展其因果AI模型，分析更多变量，运行更大的模拟，并量化营销投资中真正的增长驱动力。Alembic将成为首家使用NVIDIA DGX Vera Rubin SuperPOD进行企业级因果建模的因果AI公司，为企业高管提供关于什么推动了业务成果以及资本浪费在哪里的单一无偏真相来源，从而让他们能够对未来决策充满信心地采取行动。Alembic的推理运行在企业数据已驻留的Equinix数据中心内的私有超级计算基础设施上，保持AI工作负载本地化。World Wide Technology将此扩展至安全且受监管的环境。这些公司共同提供了一套专为对资本决策负责的高管和数据领导者构建的完整企业AI堆栈。

**拍卖速度下的智能出价**

对于广告商而言，在每天数十亿次交易中投放广告和提供相关推荐，需要准确、快速且成本足够低以大规模运行的AI。亚马逊云科技正在将云基础设施、基础模型和NVIDIA GPU加速计算整合成一个面向广告科技行业的凝聚堆栈，该堆栈能够为AI代理时代扩展规模。亚马逊云科技为广告商、需求方平台、供应方平台和独立软件供应商提供了一个生产就绪的参考实现，用于直接在拍卖内部运行AI驱动的出价——由NVIDIA Triton推理服务器提供支持，该服务器提供足够快的深度学习推理以适应实时拍卖窗口。这意味着广告技术公司可以从基于规则的决策转向基于AI模型，直接在实时拍卖管道中实现出价优化、受众激活和交易评分。

广告公司Criteo帮助零售商在正确的时间向正确的购物者展示正确的产品，覆盖数字广告领域最大的推荐网络之一。保持这些推荐的相关性意味着要持续在数十亿购物者时间线上重新训练其AI，这一过程中速度直接转化为质量。通过与NVIDIA合作，Criteo在NVIDIA Blackwell GPU上实现了约2倍的模型训练速度提升，这得益于NVIDIA cuEmbed开源库。这种效率每年已经释放了约17,000个GPU小时，并且两家公司正在进一步扩大这项工作。Taboola将相同的基础设施逻辑应用于对话式AI，使用NVIDIA GPU为其AI答案引擎DeeperDive提供动力，并将该基础设施扩展到AI平台和聊天机器人，使其能够从广告中创收。

**营销工作流中的智能体AI**

在营销和其他行业中，AI代理正越来越多地充当数字同事的角色，承担规划、执行和优化等长期任务。但这些代理只有在配备适当控制措施（包括安全护栏、可审计性和基于角色的权限）时才能为企业所部署。NVIDIA Agent Toolkit（包括NVIDIA NemoClaw蓝图和NVIDIA OpenShell安全运行时）提供了这些控制措施。例如，AI视频和图像生成生产平台Higgsfield AI提供了Higgsfield Supercomputer代理，可在单个界面中管理完整的营销自动化生命周期：从广告活动构思、规划、创意制作到发布和自主活动优化。它协调了领先的大型语言模型以及35多个图像、音频和视频模型，包括Higgsfield基于NVIDIA Blackwell架构构建的专有Soul和Soul 2.0模型。作为合作的一部分，NVIDIA Agent Toolkit软件（包括NVIDIA Nemotron开放模型）为Higgsfield Supercomputer中的专业子代理提供动力，持续在每个广告活动中运行。NemoClaw和OpenShell正在被集成以提供企业信任层。结果是：从构思和创意制作到发布、性能分析和优化的完整营销生命周期，均在单个界面中实现。财富500强中近400家公司的营销活动都是在该平台上创建的。

**规模化的上下文与内容智能**

AI在意义层面理解内容需要先进的基础设施。NVIDIA的多模态堆栈提供了向量搜索、数据处理和视频理解能力，使这种智能在生产规模下可行。AI驱动的媒体领导者KERV的Moment Match Engine评估每个视频帧和媒体资产中的大量信号，以理解单个场景、物体和产品，根据广告创意（广告的视觉和文本元素）提供内容推荐，从而提升用户参与度。KERV.ai最近优化了其处理管道，在平台中使用NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Nano Omni开放模型时，速度和效率实现了超过10倍的提升。KERV的解决方案分析每个广告或媒体简报包含的内容、与谁产生共鸣以及应在内容环境中的哪个确切时刻进行定位。在AI视频理解开放基准测试MediaPerf上，Nemotron 3 Nano Omni——已被包括使用NVIDIA DGX B200系统的PYLER在内的生态系统合作伙伴采用——在所有评估的模型（无论开源还是闭源）中实现了最高的吞吐量和最低的推理成本。

了解更多关于NVIDIA如何驱动广告和营销技术的信息。

history.title: Trump says Apple has agreed to 'build' chips with Intel — neither company confirms deal as Intel share price rockets
history.description: President Donald Trump said on Thursday that Apple has agreed to work with Intel to “design and build” chips in the United States.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 3 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter President Donald Trump said on Thursday that Apple has agreed to work with Intel to “design and build” chips in the United States, posting the claim to Truth Social before either company confirmed any arrangement. Apple and Intel didn’t respond to initial requests for comment from Reuters, and neither has issued a statement acknowledging a finalized deal.Trump says the deal is part of his administration’s push to reshore semiconductor manufacturing, writing that the U.S. needs to build its chips domestically and that he "decided to help Intel because we need to design and build our Chips right here in America.” He credited earlier government intervention for drawing Nvidia and Elon Musk's TeraFab project to Intel's foundry.Apple already designs its own silicon and has done so since it dropped Intel processors from its products in 2020. Any arrangement here would purely be a foundry deal, with Intel acting as a contract manufacturer for chips Apple designs in-house. It wouldn’t return Apple to Intel-designed processors, and it certainly wouldn’t displace TSMC from Apple's flagship products.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: The logic behind any such deal for Apple would be simple supply diversification; the company remains dependent on TSMC, whose leading-edge capacity is being absorbed by AI customers, including Nvidia and AMD. Placing a lower-volume part on a second source reduces single-foundry exposure without touching the iPhone and high-end M-series lines that rely on TSMC's most advanced nodes.We’ve known about much of this for a little while now. Reporters caught wind of a preliminary deal last month, following discussions that ran for more than a year. Before that, analyst Ming-Chi Kuo and GF Securities outlined the likely scope: Apple's M7 SoC built on Intel's 18A-P process, powering the MacBook Air and entry-level iPad Pro, with mass production targeted for late 2027. You may like Apple reportedly strikes deal for Intel to make some of its chips Apple considering Intel and Samsung for US chip production, report claims Intel joins Elon Musk's TeraFab project Those two product tiers accounted for roughly 20 million units in 2025, with annual shipments expected to settle between 15 million and 20 million — significant for a new foundry customer, but small enough to leave TSMC's revenue mix intact. Reporting has also suggested that Apple's A21 iPhone chips will move to Intel's 14A node around 2028, but none of this has been confirmed by Apple or Intel.Trump's announcement comes just two days after Intel said its 18A-P process had entered risk production, announced at the VLSI Symposium in Honolulu. The node is the first performance-enhanced version of 18A, and Intel claims it delivers 9% higher performance at the same power, or 18% lower power at the same performance, while staying design-rule compatible with 18A so customers can reuse existing IP. CEO Lip-Bu Tan told investors last month that he expects multiple foundry commitments to close in the second half of 2026.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsIntel has yet to confirm a single leading-edge customer for 18A, and any commitment from Apple would be a best-case scenario in terms of securing the validation the company both desperately wants and needs. The U.S. government holds a 10% stake in Intel, and its shares rose as much as 9% in premarket trading on the news, extending a run that has lifted the stock 464% over the past 12 months to a market cap of $608.7 billion. history.titleZh: 特朗普称苹果已同意与英特尔“共同制造”芯片——两家公司均未确认交易，英特尔股价飙升
history.descriptionZh:
美国总统唐纳德·特朗普于周四在Truth Social上发文称，苹果已同意与英特尔合作在美国“设计和制造”芯片，但该声明发布后，苹果和英特尔均未予以确认或回应。这一消息是特朗普政府推动半导体制造回流美国的举措之一，他在文中强调美国需要在本土制造芯片，并称“我决定帮助英特尔，因为我们必须在美国设计和制造芯片”。特朗普还提及此前政府干预促使英伟达和埃隆·马斯克的TeraFab项目采用英特尔的代工服务。

需要明确的是，苹果自2020年放弃英特尔处理器后，一直在自主设计芯片。因此，任何潜在的合作都纯粹是代工协议：英特尔将作为合同制造商，生产苹果自主研发的芯片，而非回归英特尔设计的处理器，更不会取代台积电在苹果旗舰产品中的地位。从苹果的角度看，此类合作的核心逻辑是供应链多元化。当前苹果高度依赖台积电，而台积电的先进产能正被英伟达、AMD等AI客户大量吸收。将部分低量产品外包给第二家代工厂，可以在不影响依赖台积电最先进制程的iPhone和高端M系列芯片的情况下，降低单一代工厂风险。

关于合作的具体规模，此前已有媒体报道。记者在上个月获悉双方初步接触，谈判持续超过一年。分析师郭明錤和GF证券此前预估了大致范围：苹果的M7系统级芯片（SoC）将采用英特尔18A-P工艺，用于MacBook Air和入门级iPad Pro，目标在2027年底实现量产。这两个产品在2025年的销量约为2000万台，预计未来年出货量稳定在1500万至2000万台之间——这对英特尔代工业务而言是重要的新客户，但对台积电营收影响有限。此外有消息称，苹果的A21 iPhone芯片可能在2028年左右转向英特尔14A节点，但以上均未得到苹果或英特尔官方确认。

特朗普发布声明两天前，英特尔在檀香山VLSI研讨会上宣布其18A-P工艺已进入风险生产阶段。这是18A的首个性能增强版本，英特尔宣称其能在相同功耗下提升9%性能，或在相同性能下降低18%功耗，同时保持设计规则与18A兼容，便于客户复用现有IP。英特尔CEO陈立武（Lip-Bu Tan）上月对投资者表示，预计2026年下半年将达成多项代工承诺。

目前，英特尔尚未公开任何18A的领先客户。若苹果最终给予承诺，将是英特尔在代工领域获得的关键性认可，这既是该公司迫切需要的，也是其梦寐以求的。美国政府持有英特尔10%的股份，受此消息影响，英特尔股价在盘前交易中一度上涨9%，延续了过去12个月的涨势（股价累计上涨464%），市值达到6087亿美元。

（注：原文中关于“加入对话”、“订阅新闻通讯”、“Tom's Hardware”等无关及宣传性内容已根据摘要要求进行过滤和整合，保留核心技术与商业信息。）

history.title: France Advances Europe’s AI Future With NVIDIA Technologies
history.description: A year ago at NVIDIA GTC Paris at VivaTech, France laid out plans to advance local AI — from new AI factories and national compute capacity to open frontier models and industrial platforms. Now, that AI infrastructure is coming online. AI agents are running in production, startups are deploying applications and the French AI ecosystem […]
history.content: A year ago at NVIDIA GTC Paris at VivaTech, France laid out plans to advance local AI — from new AI factories and national compute capacity to open frontier models and industrial platforms. Now, that AI infrastructure is coming online. AI agents are running in production, startups are deploying applications and the French AI ecosystem is developing models, datasets and platforms designed around local languages, cultural context and European requirements. French AI Infrastructure Takes Shape France’s AI ambitions are gaining momentum. Billions in investment commitments through France 2030, the 2025 AI Action Summit and this year’s Choose France Summit are reinforcing the country’s position as one of Europe’s leading destinations for AI infrastructure. As part of these efforts, Mistral is building a new 44-megawatt data center in Bruyères-le-Châtel, a commune in northern France. Announced at GTC Paris last year, Mistral’s first deployment is already operational with 18,000 NVIDIA GB200 systems — laying the foundation for the company’s roadmap of 200 megawatts of compute capacity across Europe by 2027. The NVIDIA Blackwell platform is designed to help AI factories maximize throughput within fixed power budgets, combining higher performance‑per‑watt silicon with software features that boost data center throughput in power‑constrained environments. Mistral is also working with French public investment bank Bpifrance, AI and advanced tech investment company MGX and NVIDIA to expand Campus AI, a network of AI factories anchored by a planned 1.4-gigawatt facility, making it one of Europe’s largest AI campuses. This momentum reflects a broader wave of AI infrastructure investment in France. Scaleway, a European public cloud provider, now offers NVIDIA Blackwell B300‑SXM instances, giving developers and enterprises access to accelerated computing on demand. Bull and Foxconn have announced the production of NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72 in Europe. Systems will be manufactured and initially tested at Foxconn’s facilities in the Czech Republic before being assembled, integrated and fully validated at Bull’s factory in Angers, France. And a consortium of eight leading French companies has submitted a bid to host a European AI gigafactory in France to strengthen European AI infrastructure and accelerate AI adoption. Meanwhile, Schneider Electric has teamed with NVIDIA to develop blueprints for gigawatt-scale AI factories, helping organizations accelerate AI infrastructure deployment. Open Models Underpin AI Development France’s AI ecosystem is producing models, datasets and platforms tailored to local languages, cultural context, and European business and regulatory requirements. As AI agents become more capable, organizations are increasingly adopting systems of models, using the right model for the right task to improve accuracy, reduce costs and accelerate outcomes. On stage at this year’s VivaTech event, leaders from Gradium, H Company, LINAGORA, Pleias and NVIDIA explored the role of open models in enabling more transparent, customizable and locally relevant AI for governments, enterprises and developers. “What we see now is a shift from building one isolated model to running continuous model infrastructure, where models train the next models, curate data, generate synthetic environments and verify reinforcement learning,” said Pierre-Carl Langlais, chief technology officer of Pleias. “Open model infrastructure is simply the way to ensure that many people can build AI and frontier-level practice can disseminate throughout the entire economy.” The discussion underscored a key theme: combining open models with energy‑efficient infrastructure gives organizations the control they need to inspect, adapt, deploy and audit AI that meets Europe’s compliance and trust requirements. NVIDIA Nemotron is advancing this with open models, datasets and playbooks that help model builders accelerate workflows from training to deployment. Mistral, a founding member of the NVIDIA Nemotron Coalition — a network of AI builders collaborating on open frontier models — is contributing model-development expertise and multimodal capabilities to help advance open frontier models through open collaboration. LINAGORA is building multilingual large language models with strong focus on the French language with its Luciole model family, developed using NVIDIA Nemotron and NeMo libraries and designed for local language and cultural context. Luciole 1B, 8B and 23B were pretrained on Jean-Zay — one of Europe’s most powerful and eco-efficient AI supercomputers — in collaboration with CNRS/IDRIS in the frame of the OpenLLM-France project. These open source models are distributed on Hugging Face (OpenLLM-France) along with their pretraining datasets. H Company, also part of the Nemotron Coalition, is developing Holotron, a family of AI agents built on open NVIDIA Nemotron models. These computer-use agents can interact with any software interface in the same way a human would, without needing application programming interfaces or custom integrations, and automate complex enterprise workflows from end to end. Pleias, in collaboration with NVIDIA, developed Nemotron-Personas-France and Nemotron-Personas-Belgium, privacy-preserving synthetic persona datasets grounded in French and Belgian demographics and cultural context. The startup is also using Jean Zay train compact language models entirely on open, well‑documented datasets, making it easier for customers to address EU AI Act requirements around data provenance and transparency. The team is now building specialized versions for search, retrieval-augmented generation and public sector document workflows. AI Production Pays Off The shift from pilot to production is the defining story of the past year, as organizations across every major industry in France use AI to boost efficiency, quality and speed. Initiatives like the collaboration — announced at Adopt AI — between AI Factory France (AI2F), led by GENCI, NVIDIA Inception and NVIDIA Connect programs are helping startups gain access to national supercomputing resources, including Jean Zay. Early participants, including Pleias, Nebula and Ryax Technologies, are already turning that access into deployable applications. Similar efforts are expanding across Europe, including a collaboration between NVIDIA and the Barcelona Supercomputing Center, creating a network that connects local infrastructure with startups and public sector institutions. In healthcare, Sanofi is deploying AI agents across the value chain, from research, manufacturing and commercial to daily operations like procurement and IT, helping teams automate complex workflows at global scale. The company is also working with startups Owkin and Biolevate to develop autonomous agents for drug discovery and development. Orange Business, the B2B subsidiary of telecom company Orange, adopted a lead-with-internal-use approach by first testing and scaling its Live Intelligence GenAI platform internally, with more than 100,000 active users across the company. At the same time, Orange Business made the platform available as a trusted agentic AI solution, enabling businesses and public sector organizations across Europe to adopt AI securely while keeping data hosted within the region. Stellantis announced a strategic initiative to advance AI-enabled digital twins across its global manufacturing footprint, powered by real-time data, simulation and AI, to improve efficiency, quality and operational decision-making. Dassault Systèmes is combining virtual twins with AI infrastructure and open models on its agentic 3DEXPERIENCE platform, powered by science-validated industry world models that enable the design, simulation and operation of complex systems with confidence. This establishes a secure and trustworthy foundation for industrial AI, helping scale innovation across the generative economy. TotalEnergies is building Pangea 5, a next-generation supercomputer developed with Dell Technologies and NVIDIA that will increase the company’s computing power to support seismic imaging, advanced simulation and AI-driven research in the energy sector. L’Oréal is using its CreAltech platform to combine generative AI and 3D digital twins, helping creative teams scale content production while maintaining brand consistency, quality and responsible AI practices across global markets. France’s trajectory has moved from announcing its AI ambitions to deploying the infrastructure, models and applications needed to realize them. As new AI factories come online and adoption accelerates across industries, the country is emerging as one of Europe’s most active environments for AI development. The foundations are in place. What gets built on top of them is just getting started. Join NVIDIA at VivaTech 2026 in Paris, running June 17-20.
history.author: Nat Ives
history.titleZh: 法国借助NVIDIA技术推进欧洲AI未来
history.descriptionZh:
一年前，在巴黎VivaTech举办的英伟达GTC大会上，法国公布了推进本土AI的计划——从建设新的AI工厂和国家计算能力，到开发开放前沿模型和工业平台。如今，这些AI基础设施正在逐步上线：AI智能体已投入生产运行，初创企业正在部署应用，法国AI生态系统正在围绕本地语言、文化背景和欧洲要求开发模型、数据集和平台。

**法国AI基础设施成形**

法国的AI雄心正在加速。通过“法国2030”计划、2025年AI行动峰会以及今年“选择法国”峰会承诺的数十亿欧元投资，法国正巩固其作为欧洲AI基础设施领先目的地的地位。作为这些努力的一部分，Mistral正在法国北部城镇Bruyères-le-Châtel建设一座44兆瓦的数据中心。去年在GTC巴黎大会上宣布的Mistral首个部署已经投入运营，包含18,000套英伟达GB200系统——这为该公司到2027年在欧洲达到200兆瓦计算能力的路线图奠定了基础。英伟达Blackwell平台旨在帮助AI工厂在固定功耗预算内最大化吞吐量，通过将更高性能/功耗比的芯片与软件功能相结合，在功耗受限环境中提升数据中心吞吐量。Mistral还正在与法国公共投资银行Bpifrance、AI与先进技术投资公司MGX以及英伟达合作，扩展Campus AI——一个由计划中的1.4吉瓦设施支撑的AI工厂网络，使其成为欧洲最大的AI园区之一。

这一势头反映了法国AI基础设施投资的更广泛浪潮。欧洲公有云提供商Scaleway现已提供英伟达Blackwell B300-SXM实例，使开发者和企业能够按需访问加速计算。Bull和富士康已宣布在欧洲生产英伟达Vera Rubin NVL72系统——这些系统将在富士康捷克共和国工厂制造并初步测试，然后在法国昂热的Bull工厂组装、集成并全面验证。由八家法国领先公司组成的联合体已提交投标，希望在法国主办欧洲AI超级工厂，以加强欧洲AI基础设施并加速AI采用。与此同时，施耐德电气与英伟达合作，为吉瓦级AI工厂开发蓝图，帮助组织加速AI基础设施部署。

**开放模型支撑AI发展**

法国的AI生态系统正在生产针对本地语言、文化背景以及欧洲商业和监管要求量身定制的模型、数据集和平台。随着AI智能体能力越来越强，组织越来越多地采用“模型系统”——为正确任务使用正确模型，以提高准确性、降低成本并加速成果。在今年的VivaTech活动舞台上，来自Gradium、H Company、LINAGORA、Pleias和英伟达的领导者探讨了开放模型在实现更透明、可定制和本地相关AI方面对政府、企业和开发者的作用。

“我们现在看到的转变是从构建孤立的单一模型转向运行持续的模型基础设施——模型训练下一个模型，策展数据，生成合成环境并验证强化学习，”Pleias首席技术官Pierre-Carl Langlais说。“开放模型基础设施就是确保许多人能够构建AI，并且前沿实践能够渗透到整个经济中的方式。”讨论强调了关键主题：将开放模型与节能基础设施相结合，使组织获得所需的控制权，以检查、适配、部署和审计符合欧洲合规和信任要求的AI。英伟达Nemotron正在通过开放模型、数据集和操作手册推动这一点，帮助模型构建者加速从训练到部署的工作流程。Mistral作为英伟达Nemotron联盟（一个协作开放前沿模型的AI构建者网络）的创始成员，正在贡献模型开发专业知识和多模态能力，通过开放协作推动开放前沿模型的发展。

LINAGORA正在构建多语言大型语言模型，特别注重法语，其Luciole模型系列使用英伟达Nemotron和NeMo库开发，针对本地语言和文化背景设计。Luciole 1B、8B和23B模型在Jean-Zay（欧洲最强大、最生态高效的AI超级计算机之一）上预训练，与CNRS/IDRIS在OpenLLM-France项目框架下合作完成。这些开源模型及其预训练数据集已在Hugging Face（OpenLLM-France）上发布。同样属于Nemotron联盟的H Company正在开发Holotron——基于开放英伟达Nemotron模型的AI智能体系列。这些“计算机使用”智能体能够像人类一样与任何软件界面交互，无需应用程序编程接口或自定义集成，并能端到端地自动化复杂的企业工作流程。Pleias与英伟达合作开发了Nemotron-Personas-France和Nemotron-Personas-Belgium——基于法国和比利时人口统计及文化背景的隐私保护合成人物数据集。这家初创公司还利用Jean-Zay在完全开放、记录完善的数据集上训练紧凑型语言模型，使客户更容易满足欧盟AI法案关于数据来源和透明度的要求。该团队目前正在为搜索、检索增强生成和公共部门文档工作流构建专用版本。

**AI生产见成效**

从试点到生产的转变是过去一年标志性的故事，法国各大行业组织都在利用AI提升效率、质量和速度。像AI Factory France（由GENCI领导）与英伟达Inception和英伟达Connect计划在“采纳AI”大会上宣布的合作，正在帮助初创企业获得包括Jean-Zay在内的国家超级计算资源。Pleias、Nebula和Ryax Technologies等早期参与者已将这些资源转化为可部署的应用。类似努力正在欧洲扩展，包括英伟达与巴塞罗那超级计算中心的合作，创建了一个连接本地基础设施与初创企业和公共机构网络的体系。

在医疗健康领域，赛诺菲正将AI智能体部署到从研发、制造、商业到日常运营（如采购和IT）的整个价值链中，帮助团队在全球范围内自动化复杂工作流程。该公司还正在与初创公司Owkin和Biolevate合作，开发用于药物发现和开发的自主智能体。Orange Business（电信公司Orange的B2B子公司）采取了“先内部使用”的方法，首先在公司内部测试并扩展其Live Intelligence GenAI平台，已有超过10万活跃用户。同时，Orange Business将该平台作为可信的智能体AI解决方案提供，使欧洲企业和公共部门组织能够在数据托管在区域内的情况下安全采用AI。Stellantis宣布了一项战略计划，在其全球制造网络中推进AI驱动的数字孪生，基于实时数据、仿真和AI，以提升效率、质量和运营决策。达索系统正在将其虚拟孪生与AI基础设施和开放模型结合，部署在其智能体化3DEXPERIENCE平台上，该平台由经过科学验证的行业世界模型支持，能够自信地设计、仿真和运营复杂系统。这为工业AI建立了安全可信的基础，有助于在生成式经济中扩展创新。道达尔能源正在构建Pangea 5——与戴尔科技和英伟达合作开发的下一代超级计算机，将提升公司的计算能力，以支持能源领域的 seismic 成像、先进仿真和AI驱动研究。欧莱雅正在使用其CreAltech平台，将生成式AI与3D数字孪生相结合，帮助创意团队在保持品牌一致性、质量和负责任AI实践的同时，在全球市场扩展内容生产。

法国的轨迹已从宣布其AI雄心转向部署实现这些雄心所需的基础设施、模型和应用。随着新的AI工厂陆续上线，各行业加速采用，法国正成为欧洲最活跃的AI发展环境之一。根基已奠定，在此基础上构建的成果才刚刚开始。欢迎参加2026年6月17日至20日在巴黎举行的英伟达VivaTech大会。
